content_en:
In reply to that, ~ Ali said: "Whomever ~Umar appointed as governor in provinces would also be put under close check, if he found anyone of them erring, he would never leave him unpunished. You have never done such thing, you are too lenient on your relatives."

*Uthmaan said, "They are also your relatives".

“Ali replied: "I give them no privilege above being relatives".

“Uthmaan: "Have you forgotten that “Umar mad Mu‘ awiyah a governor in a province and never ousted him throughout his Caliphate's era? So I did the same.”

“Ali: "You are right but I'd like to remind you that Mu‘ awiyah always held Umar in awe more than Umar's lad, Yarfa’, did !"

~Uthmaan, "Yes, I know that."

~Ali: "If you know that, why then give Mu’ awiyah free rein to do whatever he likes, issuing decisions in your name and you acquiesce in that?’

In addition to all what he said as factors that led to the escalation of the strife, there are other reasons, such as:

A very controversial doctrine introduced by a virtuous Companion, Abu Dharr Al-Ghifaari, concerning the distribution of wealth, sparked off a frenzied debate between him and Mu*awiyah. While Abu Dharr adopted a_ socialist-like approach, claiming that the wealth should be equally distributed among all Muslims, for they owned this wealth, Mu*awiyah,

* He was “Uthmaan’s cousin appointed as governor of Al-Basrah in the year 29 AH.

the then governor of the Levant, opposed that view raising the slogan "Allah is the Owner of all things"!

To this Abu Dharr counter-argued: "This sounds as someone who wants to manipulates the whole thing! Who says that Allah is not the Owner of all things?! But what I'm saying is that the state's wealth belongs to all Muslims and they should all have equal share or right to it." That is why Abu Dharr never supported the idea of amassing wealth, rather he would go for the idea that having a daily sustenance would suffice a Muslim, nothing more save what one reserves to spend in Allah's Cause. In supporting his argument, he used to cite the following verse:

"And there are those who bury gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah: announce unto them a most grievous penalty." (At-Tawbah: 34)

And he used to call upon the well-off class of people: "Give a due consideration to the poor (by giving them charity)".

It should not be any wonder that such an argument claimed many support among the poor class of the society, this made them too demanding and a pain in the neck of the rich who, having their backs to the wall, had no choice than to complain to the Levant's governor, Mu’ awiyah, who sent Abu Dharr back to Madeenah. Upon reaching there and confronting the Caliph, he was asked by ~Uthmaan: "What have you done to make Levantines complain a lot about you? Have you been too sharp-tongued?"

Abu Dharr replied: "Only what I said is that people should not be given free rein to amass wealth to the detriment of

others, under the slogan of "Allah is the Owner of all things".

“Uthmaan thereupon said to Abu Dharr: "My main task in this post of caliphate is to observe the obligation enjoined on me_ by Almighty Allah and to see that people also perform their duties, but not to impose an ascetic style of life on people, only what I can do is to advice them to be moderate in spending." Being dissatisfied with the answer, Abu Dharr sought the Caliph's permission to leave Madeenah. Not only that he was granted this permission, he was also honored with a befitting entourage that comprised of more than twenty camels and two servants.

Thus, the noble Companion left the Sacred City of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, and settled in a place called Ar-Rabadhah, one of the Madeenan suburbs, where he later died after two years, in the year 32 AH.

In citing this incident as one of the main factors that led to the escalation of the strife, we have two points supporting this fact: First of all, Though Abu Dharr voice his own opinion on the distribution of wealth and left, the man really left behind huge numbers of supporters (the poor class) who found it an opportunity to vex their angers against people who were given to opulence and amassing wealth to the detriment of the majority of the populace. Hence, this incident drove the whole atmosphere to the boiling point in the sense that several revolutions were staged against the province's governors basked in cream of wealth, and this eventually led to a huge uprising against the Caliph himself who acquiesced in this extravagant habits. The second point is that this idea (fair distribution of wealth) was not a carbon copy of someone's imagination or say that he was just reiterating an opinion already voiced bya Persian man, ~*Abdullaah Ibn Saba’, rather, it was a refraction of rays of Islamic noble teachings laid down by the great tutor, Prophet Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him.

So it should not be a wonder to hear a virtuous Companion like Abu Dharr Al-Ghifaari voicing a personal opinion that greatly moves along the line with the teachings of

Islam and backing it with convincing proofs from the Glorious Quran !!

Who Is ‘Abdullaah Ibn Saba'? And What Is His Relation to the Sedition?

As for the first question, he is said to be a Jew from (the tribe of Himyar. His mother was a black slave, that is why he was called ‘Ibn as-Sawaad' (the son of blackness). * Abdullaah Ibn Saba’ embraced Islam during the rule of *Uthmaan, yet strong doubts surrounded his conversion. A modern researcher stated that his conversion to Islam was instigated by a clandestine group aiming at either of two things: dividing Muslims as regards religion or politics for his mission combined belief in both nomination of the caliph "Wisiyyah" and the return of the Prophet "Raj*ah". The former requires that it is none other than * Ali who should be appointed a caliph and that *Uthmaan should give up the caliphate by rebellion against him. The latter means the belief that Prophet Muhammad will come back again to life just as Jesus, peace be upon him, will.

As for his connection with the sedition, it was the strongest, most serious and influential in respect to the incidents and developments of the sedition. Ibn Saba' started off in Basrah where he propagated his wicked mission among all groups of people. Among his sayings which constitute the basis of his mission are: "Each Prophet has Wasiyy (a nominated successor) and Ali is the successor nominated by Muhammad, so who, then, does more wrong than one who does not abide by the nomination of the Prophet?" and "* Uthmaan illegitimately seized it (the caliphate), so you should revolt against this, starting by laying accusations against your leaders and pretending that you are enjoining good and forbidding evil." Ibn Saba' also attacked the great companions; he was the first to accuse Abu Bakr and ‘Umar and degrade them. Yet, * Ali

dissociated himself from Ibn Saba' and accused him of lying against Allah and His Messenger. [bn Saba’, however, continued his deviate claims to the extent that he claimed, "It is strange for one to believe that Jesus will descend and deny that Muhammad will return" deceiving people by searching for proof (for his claim) in Allah's saying,

(NO aay "otha St Sat) OT al ale (o5) wall Or

"Verily He Who ordained the Quran for thee, will bring thee back to the Place of Return." (Al-Qasas: 85)

Once the governor of Basrah knew about his claims, he dismissed him. Ibn Saba’ therefore headed for Kufah where he resumed his destructive activity. Yet, soon the Kufah's governor dismissed him, too. He next went to Syria. By that time, he already poisoned the atmosphere in Iraq spreading such evil thoughts that deeply affected many people whose faith was still frail even though they were Muslims.

In Syria, Ibn Saba’ could hardly do anything. That was because Syria was under a_ strong, intelligent and far-sighted governor, namely Mu‘awiyah Ibn Abi Sufyaan. Ibn Saba' continued his devilish journey to Egypt where he found a fertile soil for his lies. There he incited the public against the caliph and aroused bitter feelings in their breasts against him. The absence of Egypt's governor *Abdullaah Ibn Sa*d Ibn Abi Sarh, who was preoccupied with fighting the Romans and the people of Nubia, made his task in Egypt an easy one. Perhaps the fact that Ibn Abi Sarh was callous in collecting taxes and asking Egyptians to pay more than the obligatory sum played an important role in stirring hatred and resentment against him.

In propagandizing his ideas, Ibn Saba’ made use of two men from Quraysh: Abu Hudhayfah and Muhammad Ibn Abi Bakr. The former was hostile to ~ Uthmaan because he did not appoint him for any administrative office though ~ Uthmaan took upon himself the responsibility of fostering and raising him after the death of his father. But why did the caliph refuse to confer on him a governmental office? Some narrators point: "This is because he was not an eligible candidate for authority in the eyes of the caliph who knew that his foster-son had once drunk wine. He (‘Uthmaan) openly said to him: ‘Had you been eligible, | would have appointed you, but you are not.' " As for the latter, he closely related to ~ Ali Ibn Abi Taalib who married his mother, Asmaa' Bint Abi ~Umays, after the death of Abu Bakr. Thus, he was raised in the house of his stepfather. Ibn Abi Bakr's enthusiasm for ~ Ali therefore was the reason why he supported Ibn Saba' and teamed up with against the caliph.

That way, the front opposing the caliph was stretching even though their activities remained covert until then (i.e. 34 AH). These activities took the form of mutual messages between the indignant persons here and there which included censure of the caliph and his officers in all provinces and mentioned certain attitudes that they regarded inconvenient. The voice of their censure gradually grew louder and was frequently discussed by people until it reached the caliph himself. ~Uthmaan, in turn, delegated four of the great companions to discuss these issues and search for the real reasons behind complaints filed by inhabitants of different provinces. He sent Usamah Ibn Zayd to Basrah, Muhammad Ibn Maslamah to Kufah, ~ Abdullaah Ibn ‘Umar to Syria, and ~ Ammaar Ibn Yasir to Egypt. The first three delegates returned to the capital and found that there was nothing to justify these complaints, but ~Ammaar stayed in Egypt and its governor, Ibn Abi Sarh, sent to ~Uthmaan informing him that ~Ammaar was drawn to some people in Egypt and that they managed to sway him to their
content_id:
Menjawab hal itu, Ali berkata: "Siapa pun yang diangkat ~Umar sebagai gubernur di provinsi-provinsi juga akan diawasi dengan ketat, jika ia mendapati salah seorang dari mereka berbuat kesalahan, ia tidak pernah membiarkannya tanpa hukuman. Engkau tidak pernah melakukan hal seperti itu, engkau terlalu lunak terhadap kerabatmu."

Uthmaan berkata, "Mereka juga kerabatmu".

Ali menjawab: "Aku tidak memberi mereka keistimewaan apa pun selain karena hubungan kekerabatan".

Uthmaan: "Sudahkah engkau lupa bahwa Umar menjadikan Mu'awiyah sebagai gubernur di sebuah provinsi dan tidak pernah memecatnya sepanjang era Khilafahnya? Maka aku pun melakukan hal yang sama."

Ali: "Engkau benar, tetapi aku ingin mengingatkanmu bahwa Mu'awiyah selalu merasa segan kepada Umar melebihi keseganan pelayan Umar sendiri, Yarfa', kepadanya!"

Uthmaan, "Ya, aku tahu itu."

Ali: "Jika engkau tahu itu, mengapa engkau memberi Mu'awiyah kebebasan penuh untuk melakukan apa saja yang ia kehendaki, mengeluarkan keputusan atas namamu sedangkan engkau hanya berdiam diri?"

Selain semua yang ia sebutkan sebagai faktor-faktor yang menyebabkan meningkatnya fitnah, terdapat pula sebab-sebab lain, di antaranya:

Suatu doktrin yang sangat kontroversial yang diperkenalkan oleh seorang Sahabat yang mulia, Abu Dharr Al-Ghifaari, mengenai distribusi kekayaan, memicu perdebatan sengit antara dirinya dan Mu'awiyah. Sementara Abu Dharr mengambil pendekatan yang menyerupai sosialisme, dengan mengklaim bahwa kekayaan harus dibagi secara merata di antara seluruh kaum muslimin, karena merekalah pemilik kekayaan tersebut, Mu'awiyah,

* Beliau adalah sepupu Uthmaan yang diangkat sebagai gubernur Al-Basrah pada tahun 29 H.

gubernur Syam saat itu, menentang pandangan tersebut dengan mengusung slogan "Allah adalah Pemilik segala sesuatu"!

Menanggapi hal ini, Abu Dharr berargumen: "Ini terdengar seperti seseorang yang ingin memanipulasi seluruh perkara! Siapa yang mengatakan bahwa Allah bukan Pemilik segala sesuatu?! Tetapi yang aku katakan adalah bahwa kekayaan negara adalah milik seluruh kaum muslimin dan mereka semua seharusnya mendapat bagian atau hak yang sama atasnya." Itulah sebabnya Abu Dharr tidak pernah mendukung gagasan penumpukan harta, sebaliknya ia berpendirian bahwa rezeki harian sudah cukup bagi seorang muslim, tidak lebih dari itu kecuali apa yang disisihkan untuk diinfakkan di jalan Allah. Dalam mendukung argumennya, ia sering mengutip ayat berikut:

"Dan orang-orang yang menyimpan emas dan perak serta tidak menginfakkannya di jalan Allah, maka beritakanlah kepada mereka (bahwa mereka akan mendapat) azab yang pedih." (At-Tawbah: 34)

Dan ia selalu menyerukan kepada golongan orang-orang berada: "Perhatikanlah keadaan orang-orang miskin (dengan memberi mereka sedekah)".

Tidaklah mengherankan bahwa argumen semacam itu mendapat banyak dukungan dari kalangan masyarakat miskin, sehingga membuat mereka semakin banyak menuntut dan menjadi beban bagi kaum kaya yang, terpojok dalam keadaan sulit, tidak punya pilihan selain mengadu kepada gubernur Syam, Mu'awiyah, yang kemudian mengirim Abu Dharr kembali ke Madeenah. Setibanya di sana dan berhadapan dengan Khalifah, Uthmaan bertanya kepadanya: "Apa yang telah engkau perbuat sehingga penduduk Syam banyak mengadukanmu? Apakah engkau terlalu tajam berbicara?"

Abu Dharr menjawab: "Aku hanya mengatakan bahwa orang-orang tidak boleh diberi kebebasan untuk menumpuk harta dengan merugikan

orang lain, dengan berlindung di balik slogan 'Allah adalah Pemilik segala sesuatu'".

Uthmaan kemudian berkata kepada Abu Dharr: "Tugas utamaku dalam jabatan khilafah ini adalah mengamati kewajiban yang diperintahkan Allah Yang Maha Agung kepadaku dan memastikan bahwa orang-orang pun menunaikan kewajiban mereka, bukan memaksakan gaya hidup zuhud kepada manusia, yang bisa aku lakukan hanyalah menasihati mereka untuk bersikap moderat dalam berbelanja." Tidak puas dengan jawaban tersebut, Abu Dharr meminta izin kepada Khalifah untuk meninggalkan Madeenah. Tidak hanya izin tersebut dikabulkan, ia pun dihormati dengan iring-iringan yang layak yang terdiri dari lebih dari dua puluh unta dan dua pelayan.

Demikianlah, Sahabat yang mulia ini meninggalkan Kota Suci Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, dan menetap di suatu tempat bernama Ar-Rabadhah, salah satu pinggiran kota Madeenah, di mana ia kemudian wafat dua tahun kemudian, pada tahun 32 H.

Dalam menyebutkan peristiwa ini sebagai salah satu faktor utama yang menyebabkan meningkatnya fitnah, terdapat dua poin yang mendukung fakta ini: Pertama, meskipun Abu Dharr menyuarakan pendapatnya sendiri mengenai distribusi kekayaan dan kemudian pergi, ia sesungguhnya meninggalkan sejumlah besar pendukung (golongan miskin) yang menjadikannya sebagai kesempatan untuk melampiaskan kemarahan mereka terhadap orang-orang yang gemar bermewah-mewah dan menumpuk harta dengan merugikan sebagian besar penduduk. Oleh karena itu, peristiwa ini mendorong seluruh suasana menuju titik didih dalam pengertian bahwa beberapa revolusi dilancarkan terhadap para gubernur provinsi yang tenggelam dalam kemewahan harta, dan ini akhirnya berujung pada pemberontakan besar terhadap Khalifah sendiri yang membiarkan kebiasaan berlebih-lebihan ini. Poin kedua adalah bahwa gagasan ini (distribusi kekayaan yang adil) bukanlah tiruan dari imajinasi seseorang atau bahwa ia sekadar mengulang pendapat yang sudah disuarakan oleh seorang pria Persia, Abdullaah Ibn Saba', melainkan merupakan pantulan dari cahaya ajaran Islam yang mulia yang diletakkan oleh guru agung, Nabi Muhammad, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam.

Maka tidak mengherankan untuk mendengar seorang Sahabat mulia seperti Abu Dharr Al-Ghifaari menyuarakan pendapat pribadi yang sangat selaras dengan ajaran

Islam dan menguatkannya dengan dalil-dalil yang meyakinkan dari Al-Quran Al-Karim!!

Siapakah Abdullaah Ibn Saba'? Dan Apa Hubungannya dengan Fitnah?

Adapun pertanyaan pertama, ia disebutkan sebagai seorang Yahudi dari suku Himyar. Ibunya adalah seorang budak berkulit hitam, itulah mengapa ia dijuluki 'Ibn as-Sawaad' (putra dari kegelapan). Abdullaah Ibn Saba' memeluk Islam pada masa pemerintahan Uthmaan, namun keislamannya dilingkupi keraguan yang kuat. Seorang peneliti modern menyatakan bahwa keislamannya dipicu oleh sebuah kelompok rahasia yang bertujuan pada salah satu dari dua hal: memecah belah kaum muslimin dalam hal agama atau politik, karena misinya menggabungkan keyakinan tentang wasiyat pengangkatan Khalifah "Wisiyyah" dan keyakinan tentang kembalinya Nabi "Raj'ah". Yang pertama mensyaratkan bahwa tidak lain Ali-lah yang seharusnya diangkat sebagai Khalifah dan bahwa Uthmaan harus menyerahkan khilafah melalui pemberontakan terhadapnya. Yang kedua berarti keyakinan bahwa Nabi Muhammad akan kembali hidup lagi sebagaimana Isa, alaihissalam, akan kembali.

Adapun hubungannya dengan fitnah, ia adalah yang terkuat, paling serius, dan paling berpengaruh sehubungan dengan peristiwa dan perkembangan fitnah tersebut. Ibn Saba' memulai gerakannya di Basrah di mana ia menyebarkan misi jahatnya di antara semua lapisan masyarakat. Di antara ucapannya yang menjadi landasan misinya adalah: "Setiap Nabi memiliki Washiyy (penerus yang ditunjuk) dan Ali adalah penerus yang ditunjuk oleh Muhammad, maka siapakah yang lebih zalim dari orang yang tidak mengikuti penunjukan Nabi tersebut?" dan "Uthmaan telah merebut jabatan (khilafah) secara tidak sah, maka kalian harus memberontak terhadapnya, dimulai dengan menjatuhkan tuduhan kepada pemimpin-pemimpin kalian dan berpura-pura bahwa kalian sedang menegakkan amar ma'ruf dan nahi munkar." Ibn Saba' juga menyerang para Sahabat yang agung; dialah orang pertama yang menuduh Abu Bakr dan Umar serta merendahkan keduanya. Namun, Ali

berlepas diri dari Ibn Saba' dan menuduhnya berdusta atas nama Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Ibn Saba' terus saja melancarkan klaim-klaim sesatnya hingga ia mengklaim, "Sungguh aneh bagi seseorang yang mempercayai bahwa Isa akan turun lagi namun mengingkari bahwa Muhammad akan kembali," ia menipu orang-orang dengan mencari dalil (untuk klaimnya) dalam firman Allah,

(NO aay "otha St Sat) OT al ale (o5) wall Or

"Sesungguhnya yang mewajibkan atasmu (melaksanakan hukum-hukum) Al-Quran, benar-benar akan mengembalikan kamu ke tempat kembali." (Al-Qasas: 85)

Begitu gubernur Basrah mengetahui tentang klaim-klaimnya, ia mengusirnya. Maka Ibn Saba' menuju Kufah di mana ia melanjutkan aktivitas destruktifnya. Namun, tidak lama kemudian gubernur Kufah pun mengusirnya. Ia kemudian pergi ke Syam. Pada saat itu, ia telah berhasil meracuni suasana di Irak dengan menyebarkan pemikiran-pemikiran sesat yang sangat mempengaruhi banyak orang yang imannya masih lemah meskipun mereka adalah kaum muslimin.

Di Syam, Ibn Saba' hampir tidak bisa berbuat apa-apa. Hal ini dikarenakan Syam berada di bawah seorang gubernur yang kuat, cerdas, dan berpandangan jauh ke depan, yaitu Mu'awiyah Ibn Abi Sufyaan. Ibn Saba' melanjutkan perjalanan setan ke Mesir di mana ia mendapati lahan yang subur bagi kedustaannya. Di sana ia menghasut masyarakat umum melawan Khalifah dan membangkitkan perasaan pahit dalam dada mereka terhadapnya. Ketidakhadiran gubernur Mesir, Abdullaah Ibn Sa'd Ibn Abi Sarh, yang tengah sibuk berperang melawan bangsa Romawi dan penduduk Nubia, mempermudah tugasnya di Mesir. Barangkali fakta bahwa Ibn Abi Sarh bersikap keras dalam memungut pajak dan meminta penduduk Mesir membayar lebih dari jumlah yang wajib memainkan peran penting dalam membakar kebencian dan rasa dendam terhadapnya.

Dalam mempropagandakan ide-idenya, Ibn Saba' memanfaatkan dua orang dari Quraysh: Abu Hudhayfah dan Muhammad Ibn Abi Bakr. Yang pertama menaruh permusuhan terhadap Uthmaan karena ia tidak mengangkatnya untuk jabatan administratif mana pun meskipun Uthmaan telah mengambil tanggung jawab untuk mengasuh dan membesarkannya setelah kematian ayahnya. Tetapi mengapa Khalifah menolak memberikan jabatan pemerintahan kepadanya? Beberapa perawi menyebutkan: "Ini karena ia dipandang tidak layak untuk memegang kekuasaan di mata Khalifah yang mengetahui bahwa anak asuhnya itu pernah meminum khamar. Ia (Uthmaan) terang-terangan berkata kepadanya: 'Seandainya engkau layak, tentu aku akan mengangkatmu, tetapi engkau tidak layak.'" Adapun yang kedua, ia memiliki hubungan dekat dengan Ali Ibn Abi Taalib yang menikahi ibunya, Asmaa' Bint Abi Umays, setelah wafatnya Abu Bakr. Dengan demikian, ia dibesarkan di rumah ayah tirinya. Antusiasme Ibn Abi Bakr terhadap Ali itulah yang menjadi alasan mengapa ia mendukung Ibn Saba' dan bersekutu dengannya melawan Khalifah.

Dengan demikian, front yang menentang khalifah semakin meluas meskipun aktivitas mereka masih bersifat tersembunyi hingga saat itu (yaitu tahun 34 H). Aktivitas-aktivitas ini berbentuk saling berkirim pesan antara orang-orang yang tidak puas di berbagai tempat, yang berisi kecaman terhadap khalifah dan para pejabatnya di seluruh provinsi serta menyebutkan sikap-sikap tertentu yang mereka anggap tidak patut. Suara kecaman mereka perlahan-lahan semakin lantang dan sering diperbincangkan oleh masyarakat hingga sampai ke telinga khalifah sendiri. ~Utsmaan kemudian mengutus empat orang sahabat besar untuk membahas persoalan-persoalan ini dan mencari tahu alasan sebenarnya di balik keluhan-keluhan yang diajukan oleh penduduk berbagai provinsi. Beliau mengutus Usamah Ibn Zayd ke Basrah, Muhammad Ibn Maslamah ke Kufah, ~Abdullah Ibn 'Umar ke Syam, dan ~Ammaar Ibn Yasir ke Mesir. Ketiga utusan pertama kembali ke ibu kota dan mendapati bahwa tidak ada alasan yang membenarkan keluhan-keluhan tersebut, namun ~Ammaar tetap tinggal di Mesir dan gubernurnya, Ibn Abi Sarh, mengirim surat kepada ~Utsmaan memberitahukan bahwa ~Ammaar tertarik kepada sekelompok orang di Mesir dan bahwa mereka berhasil mempengaruhi dirinya ke arah
content_ar: In reply to that, ~ علي said: "Whomever ~عمر appointed as governor in provinces would أيضاً be put under close check, إذا he found anyone of them erring, he would never leave him unpunished. You have never done such thing, you are أيضاً lenient on your relatives." *Uthmaan said, "They are أيضاً your relatives". “علي replied: "I give them no privilege above being relatives". “Uthmaan: "Have you forgotten that “عمر mad Mu‘ awiyah a governor in a province and never ousted him throughout his الخلافة's era? لذا I did the same.” “علي: "You are الحق but I'd like to remind you that Mu‘ awiyah always held عمر in awe more than عمر's lad, Yarfa’, did !" ~Uthmaan, "Yes, I know that." ~علي: "إذا you know that, why then give Mu’ awiyah free rein to do whatever he likes, issuing decisions in your name and you acquiesce in that?’ بالإضافة إلى all what قال as factors that led to the escalation of the strife, there are other reasons, مثل: A very controversial doctrine introduced by a virtuous Companion, Abu Dharr Al-Ghifaari, فيما يتعلق بـ the distribution of الثروة, sparked off a frenzied debate between him and Mu*awiyah. بينما Abu Dharr adopted a_ socialist-like approach, claiming that the الثروة should be equally distributed among all المسلمون, for they owned this الثروة, Mu*awiyah, * He was “Uthmaan’s cousin appointed as governor of Al-Basrah in the year 29 AH. the then governor of the Levant, opposed that view raising the slogan "الله is the Owner of all things"! To this Abu Dharr counter-argued: "This sounds as someone who wants to manipulates the whole thing! Who says that الله is not the Owner of all things?! But what I'm saying is that the state's الثروة belongs to all المسلمون and they should all have equal share or الحق to it." That is why Abu Dharr never supported the idea of amassing الثروة, rather he would go for the idea that having a daily sustenance would suffice a مسلم, nothing more save what one reserves to spend in الله's Cause. In supporting his argument, he used to cite the following الآية: "And there are those who bury gold and silver and spend it not in the الطريق of الله: announce unto them a most grievous penalty." (At-التوبة: 34) And he used to call upon the well-off class of people: "Give a due consideration to the poor (by giving them الصدقة)". It should not be any wonder that such an argument claimed many support among the poor class of the المجتمع, this made them أيضاً demanding and a الألم in the neck of the rich who, having their backs to the wall, had no choice than to complain to the Levant's governor, Mu’ awiyah, who sent Abu Dharr back to Madeenah. Upon reaching there and confronting the Caliph, he was asked by ~Uthmaan: "What have you done to make Levantines complain a lot about you? Have you been أيضاً sharp-tongued?" Abu Dharr replied: "Only what I said is that people should not be given free rein to amass الثروة
